---
title: "Mechanical_Work_Code"
author: "zach"
date: "2026-03-01"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r Load and Combine Subjects}

library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)

# -----------------------------
# Step 0: Load your combined data
# -----------------------------
combined_subject_data_clean <- read_csv("C:/Users/zzegu/Documents/Visual3DExample1/Combined_Subjects/combined_subject_data_clean.csv")

# -----------------------------
# Step 1: Define transition steps
# -----------------------------
transition_steps <- tibble(
  Subject = 1:20,
  StartStep = c(26, 21, 25, 24, 29, 28, 34, 28, 25, 26,
                30, 35, 26, 30, 29, 31, 29, 28, 28, 29),
  StopStep = c(30, 29, 32, 29, 48, 33, 40, 35, 35, 33,
               45, 34, 32, 37, 35, 37, 34, 44, 34, 35)
)

# -----------------------------
# Step 2: Clean subject labels & join steps
# -----------------------------
clean_data <- combined_subject_data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Subject = str_extract(File, "(?i)s\\d+") %>%
              str_remove_all("(?i)s") %>%
              as.numeric(),
    FilePart = case_when(
      str_detect(File, "_start") ~ "start",
      str_detect(File, "_stop") ~ "stop",
      TRUE ~ "unknown"
    )
  ) %>%
  left_join(transition_steps, by = "Subject")

# -----------------------------
# Step 3: Create Period variable
# -----------------------------
clean_data <- clean_data %>%
  mutate(
    Period = case_when(
      FilePart == "start" & Step >= (StartStep - 5) & Step < StartStep ~ "Late Baseline",
      FilePart == "start" & Step >= StartStep & Step <= (StartStep + 5) ~ "Early Adaptation",
      FilePart == "stop"  & Step >= (StopStep - 5) & Step < StopStep ~ "Late Adaptation",
      FilePart == "stop"  & Step >= StopStep & Step <= (StopStep + 5) ~ "Post Adaptation",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Period)) %>%
  mutate(Period = factor(Period,
                         levels = c("Late Baseline",
                                    "Early Adaptation",
                                    "Late Adaptation",
                                    "Post Adaptation")))

# -----------------------------
# Step 4: Pivot work variables
# -----------------------------
work_vars <- c(
  "L_Ankle_Pos_Work", "L_Ankle_Neg_Work",
  "R_Ankle_Pos_Work", "R_Ankle_Neg_Work",
  "L_Knee_Pos_Work", "L_Knee_Neg_Work",
  "R_Knee_Pos_Work", "R_Knee_Neg_Work",
  "L_Hip_Pos_Work",  "L_Hip_Neg_Work",
  "R_Hip_Pos_Work",  "R_Hip_Neg_Work"
)

data_long <- clean_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(work_vars), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Work") %>%
  group_by(Subject, Period, Variable) %>%
  summarise(Work = mean(Work, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# -----------------------------
# Step 5: Run LMM and save results
# -----------------------------
results <- list()

for (var in unique(data_long$Variable)) {
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("LMM FOR:", var, "\n")
  cat("========================================\n")

  data_var <- data_long %>% filter(Variable == var)

  model <- lmer(Work ~ Period + (1 | Subject), data = data_var)
  print(summary(model))

  # Post hoc
  em <- emmeans(model, pairwise ~ Period, adjust = "holm")
  print(em$contrasts)

  # Save
  results[[var]] <- list(model_summary = summary(model),
                         posthoc = em$contrasts)
}

# -----------------------------
# Step 6: Generate line plots with mean ± SEM
# -----------------------------
plot_data <- data_long %>%
  group_by(Variable, Period) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_Work = mean(Work),
    sem_Work  = sd(Work)/sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Period, y = mean_Work, group = Variable, color = Variable)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_Work - sem_Work, ymax = mean_Work + sem_Work), width = 0.2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Period", y = "Mean Work (J)", title = "Joint Work Across Periods") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
